equations.label: eq:damage-function
equations.latex: D_j = f(T_j, P_j;\, \theta) = \sum_{k} \theta_k \cdot \mathbf{1}\!\left[T_j \in \mathcal{B}_k\right] \cdot d_j^k, \quad \begin{bmatrix} D_j^{\text{mort}} \\ D_j^{\text{agr}} \\ D_j^{\text{ener}} \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} \theta_k^m \\ \theta_k^a \\ \theta_k^e \end{bmatrix} \cdot d_j^k
equations.description: Damage function: county-level damage $D_j$ from climate is a sum of bin-specific effects $\theta_k$ weighted by days $d_j^k$ in temperature bin $\mathcal{B}_k$.

qa.question: What does theta_k represent in the damage function?
qa.answer: The marginal damage of an additional day in temperature bin B_k

equations.label: eq:nonlinear-dose
equations.description: Panel fixed effects estimating equation: outcome $y_{ct}$ for county $c$ in year $t$, with county FE $\alpha_c$, state$\times$year FE $\gamma_{st}$, and temperature bin days $d_{ct}^k$.

equations.label: eq:panel-fe
equations.latex: y_{ct} = \alpha_c + \lambda_t + \beta T_{ct} + \gamma P_{ct} + \varepsilon_{ct}, \quad E[\varepsilon_{ct} \mid \alpha_c, \lambda_t, T_{ct}, P_{ct}] = 0
equations.description: Linear panel fixed effects: within-county, within-year identification exploits year-to-year weather fluctuations after removing permanent differences across counties.

equations.label: eq:adaptation
equations.description: Adaptation model: bin coefficient $\beta_k$ decays from short-run $\beta_k^0$ toward long-run adapted value $\beta_k^\infty$ at rate $\rho$.

equations.label: eq:projected-damages
equations.description: Projected damages: integral of estimated damage function $\hat{f}(T)$ against the difference in temperature distributions under future ($\mu_1, \sigma_1$) vs. baseline ($\mu_0, \sigma_0$) climate.

equations.label: eq:wtp
equations.latex: \\text{WTP}_j = \\frac{\\partial D_j / \\partial T_j}{\\partial U_j / \\partial I_j} = \\frac{-\\partial \\ln y_j / \\partial T_j}{\\partial \\ln y_j / \\partial I_j} \\cdot I_j
equations.description: Willingness to pay to avoid temperature increase: ratio of marginal climate damage to marginal utility of income, expressed as income share.

equations.label: eq:scc
equations.description: Social cost of carbon: discounted sum of marginal willingness to pay for climate stability, weighted by the temperature response to an additional unit of emissions $E_0$ today.

qa.question: What is the main identification strategy?
qa.answer: Within-county year-to-year weather variation, controlling for county FE and state-by-year FE

qa.question: What is the mortality effect of days above 90F in the preferred specification?
qa.answer: 1.084 deaths per 100,000 in column 4 with county FE and state-year FE and linear trends

qa.question: By what factor does the linear temperature specification understate mortality effects?
qa.answer: By a factor of 2.3 (0.481 linear vs 1.084 bins estimate)

qa.question: What is the projected mortality increase under the IPCC A1B scenario by 2050?
qa.answer: 2.481 additional deaths per 100,000 per year (central estimate)

qa.question: By approximately what percentage has temperature damage sensitivity declined over the sample period?
qa.answer: Approximately 38 percent (hot-day coefficient falls from 1.614 in 1968-1980 to 0.984 in 1994-2002)
